title: Why Micron Technology (MU) Is Up 7.5% After New U.S. DRAM Ramp For AI Data Centers
description:
Micron Technology has begun manufacturing 1-alpha DRAM at its Manassas, Virginia fab and is sampling 256GB 1-gamma DDR5 RDIMMs for AI data center platforms, advancing its multi-decade, approximately US$200.00 billion U.S. expansion plan supported by more than US$2.00 billion in local investment and extensive workforce programs.
By combining cutting-edge high-capacity DDR5 modules with expanded domestic DRAM production, Micron is tightening its integration into the AI compute stack while...

title: Micron joins $1 trillion club as AI race powers memory chip boom
description: STORY: :: MicronMicron Technology topped $1 trillion in market value for the first time on Tuesday...crowning a dizzying rally that has cemented the largest U.S. memory chipmaker as one of the standout winners of the AI boom.Micron's shares rose more than 18% on Tuesday, pushing the stock to a record high after brokerage firm UBS more than tripled its price target on the stock to over $1,600.The milestone underscores the central role of memory chips - used to store and move data - in AI infrastructure...and reflects a broadening of the AI trade beyond chipmakers like Nvidia whose processors train and run AI models.Micron's ascent also gives the U.S. a strong contender in a memory-chip race that has largely been led by Asia so far.South Korea's Samsung Electronics, the world's top memory chipmaker, has already hit the $1 trillion milestone, while the country's SK Hynix is also closing in.Chip stocks in general continued their surge on Tuesday, with Marvell Technology rising more than 10% in morning trading, while Qualcomm added as much as 8%. Shares of Broadcom and Intel were also higher.All helped push the Philadelphia Semiconductor index up nearly 6% to an all-time high.
